<!-- Generated by `npm run build:context` (GRAMMAR.md §10). Do not edit by hand. -->

# Interaction utilities

Utilities are token-bound class grammar, not hard-coded values. They live in `@layer utilities`, so unlayered consumer CSS overrides them without `!important`.

- Pattern families use `base[-edge]-{value}`. Edges are logical (`block`, `inline`, `block-start`, `inline-end`) unless a family says otherwise.
- When a family lists responsive support, prefix its class with `tablet:`, `tablet-lg:`, `desktop:`, `desktop-lg:`, or `widescreen:`.
- Negative values use `neg-{value}` only where the family offers them.
- Values resolve through design tokens; use the documented grammar instead of writing raw CSS values.

## Pointer utilities

Dictates styling and available actions for visible cursors and pointer/touch actions.

### cursor

Sets the visual display of the cursor icon.

Grammar: `cursor-{value}` · values: auto, default, pointer, wait, text, move, help, not-allowed, none, context-menu, progress, cell, crosshair, vertical-text, alias, copy, no-drop, grab, grabbing, all-scroll, col-resize, row-resize, n-resize, e-resize, s-resize, w-resize, ne-resize, nw-resize, se-resize, sw-resize, ew-resize, ns-resize, nesw-resize, nwse-resize, zoom-in, zoom-out

- Use the appropriate contextual cursor when it provides meaningful information to the end user. 'Pointer' for when the cursor is above interactive (clickable) elements, 'not-allowed' when the element is in a disabled state, 'grab' when an element is draggable, etc. Hyper specific cursors like 'vertical-text' or 'copy' are not necessary - use 'pointer' for a copy button and the default cursor for an input element or when hovering over text content instead.

### pointer-events

Determines the interactivy level of content when hovered over with a mouse.

Grammar: `pointer-events-{value}` · values: auto, none

- Use 'none' if you need an overlay or icon to not block element interactivity behind it.

### user-select

Controls whether users can select text within an element.

Grammar: `user-select-{value}` · values: auto, none, text, all

- Use 'none' on interactive items with text within them to prevent touch interaction from triggering text selection if pressed for too long. Use 'all' for bodies of copy (like a code block) that a user would conceivably want to select and copy in a full chunk.

### touch-action

Determines how the browser manages default touchscreen gestures (like panning, scrolling, and zooming) over a specific element

Grammar: `touch-action-{value}` · values: auto, none, pan-x, pan-y, pan-left, pan-right, pan-up, pan-down, pinch-zoom, manipulation

- Be wary of overusing 'none' or applying it on elements like <body> as it will disable zoom ability for users who may need it for accessibility reasons.
